titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the John Irwin Viney papers
titlestmt.author: Alison Fulmer

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.
This collection contains 22 published digital objects. And those objects are comprised of 22 digital files.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday April 27, 2026 at 23:06

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Material primarily in English. Some material in Chinese.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from William Reese Co. (Swann Galleries' sale, April 14, 2023) on the Walter Jennings Memorial Fund, 2023.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material is open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by John Irwin Viney are in the public domain. There are no restrictions on the use of these materials. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: John Irwin Viney Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: John Irwin Viney (1882-1942) was born in Liverpool, England and raised in Newport News, Virginia. He received a BA and MA from the University of Virginia and an LS from Harvard in 1905. Viney then lived in China from 1906 to 1911 to work as a student interpreter in Peking and later as deputy consul at Shanghai and Chefoo (now Yantai) during the last years of imperial rule before the 1912 proclamation of the Republic of China. Viney later returned to the United States and pursued a career as a lawyer and circuit court judge in St. Petersburg, Florida.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection primarily relates to Viney's time in China from 1906-1911 and includes correspondence, financial and legal documents, printed ephemera, manuscripts and typescripts, and a small number of photographs. Of note are correspondence and ephemera relating to several social and fraternal clubs based in China, papers on a diplomatic embezzler whom Viney escorted from China to U.S. prison in 1910, a typescript Memorandum on the Political Situation by Edward Selby Little, and printed State Department circulars and reports including a Memorandum Concerning Chefoo Foreign Settlement .

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Eleven nitrate negatives were found among the photographic materials. All negatives were digitized and then discarded following digitization in compliance with Yale University policy.

Some material originally housed in the folder "Miscellaneous manuscripts, 1909-1911, undated" has been rehoused in separate folders. See the processing information note for "Miscellaneous manuscripts" for more information.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Three documents in this folder were originally housed with material in the "Miscellaneous manuscripts, 1909-1911, undated" folder: Viney's Security for Overdrafts at the Russo-Asiatic Bank and two invoices for Viney's payments to his Chinese language teacher.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes small papers with Chinese characters printed on them for use in language learning. Some papers have handwritten English translations and pronunciations on the back and some have printed color images. This folder also includes some ephemera that was stored with the language flash cards including several of Viney's business cards.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This box includes hundreds of small papers with Chinese characters printed on them for use in language learning. Some papers have handwritten English translations and pronunciations on the back and some have printed color images.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder includes notes, stories, poetry, an insurance policy belonging to Viney, and an award documenting Viney's membership in the Harvard University Graduate Club. Note that some stories and poems contain racist and sexist language.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Some material originally housed in this folder has been reorganized into new folders including "Documents relating to Viney's consular positions, 1909-1910", "Viney's typescript translation of the Tsingtauer Neueste Nachrichten , 1910", " Memorandum on the Political Situation typescript by Edward Selby Little, 1910", and "Typescript translation of the Official Gazette by Dr. Tenney [?], March 15, 1909". Three documents were rehoused in "Invoices and accounts, circa 1907-1911": Viney's Security for Overdrafts at the Russo-Chinese (Asiatic) Bank and two invoices for Viney's payments to his Chinese language teacher.

did.unittitle: Documents relating to Viney's consular positions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Viney's Oath of Allegiance and Office for his position as Vice Consul in Chefoo (June 25, 1910) and the certificate of appointment for his position as Deputy Consul General in Shanghai (September 7, 1909).

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Material in this folder was originally housed with material in the folder "Miscellaneous manuscripts, 1909-1911, undated".

did.unittitle: Viney's typescript translation from the Tsingtauer Neueste Nachrichten

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Viney's English translation from the German-language newspaper Tsingtauer Neueste Nachrichten .

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Material in this folder was originally housed with material in the folder "Miscellaneous manuscripts, 1909-1911, undated".

did.unittitle: Memorandum on the Political Situation typescript by Edward Selby Little

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Material in this folder was originally housed with material in the folder "Miscellaneous manuscripts, 1909-1911, undated".

did.unittitle: Typescript translation from the Official Gazette by Dr. Tenney

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Material in this folder was originally housed with material in the folder "Miscellaneous manuscripts, 1909-1911, undated".

did.unittitle: Legal document concerning voter registration of Columbia University students

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Material in this folder was originally housed with material in the folder "Miscellaneous manuscripts, 1909-1911, undated".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder includes booklets of regulations, circulars, and documents pertaining to American consulates in China. Of note is a typescript Memorandum Concerning Chefoo Foreign Settlement .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder includes handwritten notes, greeting cards, invitations, booklets of regulations for volunteer organizations,and materials pertaining to various clubs, including the Lodge St. Andrew in Chefoo, the Lodge Cosmopolitan in Shanghai, and the Ancient Landmark Lodge in Shanghai.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains photographic prints of individual and group portraits.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Eleven 3.5" by 5.5" nitrate film negatives have been removed from this folder and are pending digitization.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains correspondence about the formation of the Yentai Club in Chefoo and a manuscript floor plan.
